items.title: Johor state election: EC issues 24,677 postal ballot papers
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — The Election Commission (EC) has issued 24,677 postal ballot papers to eligible voters for the 16th Johor State Election. EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the issuance of the postal ballot papers was carried out yesterday by election officials across all 56 state constituencies, as provided under Regulation 3 of the Election (Postal Voting) Regulations 2003. He said of the total, 23,288 postal ballot papers were issued to voters under the Form 1A category involving election officials, EC members and officers, police personnel, military personnel and media practitioners.

items.title: E-hailing driver pleads not guilty to molest of passenger in Melaka
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — An e-hailing driver pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Magistrates’ Court in Melaka yesterday to a charge of molesting a passenger last month. The accused, Mohd Ali Husin, 40, entered the plea before Magistrate Nur Afiqah Radhiah Zainurin, according to a report published in Harian Metro . According to the charge, Mohd Ali is alleged to have committed the offence inside a Proton Saga along Jalan Bukit Rambai, near Taman Bukit Rambai here, at about 12.40pm on May 27.

items.title: Syed Saddiq arrives at apex court for make-or-break verdict
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — Muar Member of Parliament (MP) Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman arrived at the Palace of Justice today to hear the Federal Court’s decision on whether he will remain acquitted or face the reinstatement of his conviction and sentence over four charges involving alleged misuse of Angkatan Bersatu Anak Muda (Armada) funds. Syed Saddiq, 33, arrived at 8.50 am, accompanied by his mother, Shariffah Mahani Syed Abdul Aziz, and fiancée, actress Bella Astillah. On June 25, 2025, the Court of Appeal acquitted and discharged Syed Saddiq after allowing his appeal to overturn the High Court’s earlier decision, which had sentenced him to seven years’ imprisonment, two strokes of the cane and a RM10 million fine.

items.title: How a Thai teen's night at a Pattaya beach ended in death
items.summary: In the early hours of Thursday, they returned to his apartment, where she was strangled and killed, police said. Two days later, Thai police said they found the teenager's naked corpse, stuffed inside a suitcase and dumped in waist-high grass, near a railway track, a short distance away from the beachfront. Reuters was unable to reach Carman for comment, and police said he has not yet appointed a lawyer.

items.title: Home Ministry: Deaths in immigration detention depots total 465 from 2021 to 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — A total of 465 deaths among detainees were recorded at immigration detention depots between 2021 and 2025. He said 393 cases, or 84.5 per cent, involved male detainees, while 60 cases involved female detainees. “The highest number of deaths involved Filipino nationals at 222 cases, followed by Indonesians (109 cases) and Myanmar nationals (61 cases),” he said.

items.title: Syed Saddiq arrives at Federal Court for verdict on prosecution's appeal [WATCH]
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman arrived at the Palace of Justice ahead of the Federal Court's verdict on the prosecution's appeal to reinstate his seven-year jail sentence, two strokes of the rotan and a RM10 million fine for corruption-related offences. The 34-year-old, dressed in a black suit, walked into the court complex accompanied by his family, singer-actress fiancée Bella Astillah, as well as supporters from the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda). Syed Saddiq co-founded Muda but stepped down as its president following his initial conviction in 2023.

items.title: Johor election: Tan banks on service record to retain Pekan Nanas
items.summary: PONTIAN: Barisan Nasional candidate Tan Eng Meng is no stranger to constituents in Pekan Nanas. Throughout his previous term, Tan said he had introduced various initiatives to safeguard the welfare of the people. Tan will face Pakatan Harapan candidate Yeo Tiong Siong.

items.title: Genting kidnapping case: Three South Koreans granted bail over RM39.5m crypto ransom plot, victim jailed for overstaying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Three South Korean men accused of kidnapping their compatriot in a case linked to a cryptocurrency ransom demand worth RM39.5 million were granted bail by the Sessions Court in Raub, Pahang, on Monday. According to MySinchew, Sessions Court judge Noor Zaihan Ali allowed bail at RM20,000 each with two local guarantors. The three men — Kwon Kyung-ryul, 28, Kwak Jae-il, 40, and Kim Myeong-joon, 40, all from Busan — had previously pleaded not guilty to unlawfully confining 40-year-old South Korean national Kim Chan-kyu.

items.title: Saifuddin: 465 deaths recorded at immigration depots from 2021 to 2025
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said 393 of the deaths involved male detainees, while 60 involved females. Twelve deaths involved children, comprising eight boys and four girls. The highest number of deaths involved Philippine citizens with 222 cases, followed by Indonesians (109) and Myanmar nationals (61), he said in a written parliamentary reply.

items.title: Yen hits 40-year low as clock ticks on intervention
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The yen was pinned at levels not seen since 1986 on Tuesday, stoking worries that a direct intervention from Tokyo was around the corner, while the dollar backed away from 13-month highs ahead of jobs data that could influence the US rate outlook. The euro was at US$1.14, while sterling last bought US$1.33. The Australian dollar eased 0.15 per cent to US$0.69 and the New Zealand dollar fetched US$0.56.

items.title: Viral Queen Elizabeth Hospital I scenes prompt Sabah Health Dept pledge to improve facilities, services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Sabah Health Department (JKNS) has pledged to improve facilities and service delivery at Queen Elizabeth Hospital I (HQE I) following public feedback sparked by viral social media posts showing conditions at the hospital at night. In a statement yesterday, JKNS said the concerns raised included waiting times at the Emergency and Trauma Department (ETD), members of the public resting in the hospital lobby, canteen operations and parking facilities. Addressing images showing people resting or sleeping in the hospital lobby and corridors, JKNS said checks found they were not patients waiting for treatment or appointments at the ETD.

items.title: Digital Realty to pay Blackstone US$3.5bil for stake in Virginia data centers
items.summary: NEW YORK: Digital Realty will acquire a larger stake in three data centres in Northern Virginia from asset manager Blackstone in a US$3.5 billion cash-and-stock deal, the companies said on Monday. Under the deal, which is expected to close on Tuesday, the data centre company will pay US$1.2 billion of cash and US$2.3 billion in Digital Realty shares to Blackstone's funds. The companies said Digital Realty will acquire Blackstone's 80 per cent interest in two 96-megawatt data centres in Manassas, Virginia, and a 50 per cent interest in a 96-megawatt centre in Sterling, Virginia.

items.title: #NSTviral: LRT3 effect? Social media abuzz over smoother commutes, clearer roads
items.summary: On Threads, several users said the usually busy Federal Highway looked unusually clear this morning, prompting many to link the improvement to the new rail line. wrote user @syazwanics. User @slow.coding made a similar observation, saying: "9am and the Federal Highway is green.

items.title: ‘Pray for him’: Syed Saddiq’s fiancée Bella Astillah arrives at Federal Court ahead of final appeal verdict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman’s fiancée, Bella Astillah, arrived at the Federal Court here this morning ahead of the prosecution’s final appeal against his acquittal on four charges involving criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering. A three-member bench led by Court of Appeal President Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais, sitting with Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, is scheduled to deliver its decision after previously hearing submissions from both the prosecution and defence. The proceedings mark the prosecution’s final avenue of appeal, with Syed Saddiq appearing as the respondent.

items.title: Indonesia turns to Felda playbook to transform newly secured border land
items.summary: JAKARTA, June 30 — Malaysia’s Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) has been highlighted in Indonesia’s House of Representatives (DPR) as a successful model for managing agricultural land and improving rural livelihoods, with elements of the scheme proposed for the development of newly recognised border areas. DPR Commission II oversees home affairs, regional administration, agrarian affairs, and land issues. Citing Felda as a model, Muhammad Rifqinizamy said Malaysia’s experience demonstrated how state-managed land development and plantation could improve rural livelihoods.

items.title: MARA deploys military veterans to patrol MRSM hostels in bullying crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — A total of 16 Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) veterans will report for duty as full-time wardens at eight selected MARA Junior Science Colleges (MRSMs) starting Wednesday (July 1). “For the current phase, there will be a total of 32 wardens across the eight MRSMs, comprising two male and two female wardens at each college. In the first phase, 16 male wardens will begin their duties on July 1, 2026.

items.title: Syed Saddiq ready for final verdict after six-year legal battle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman is ready to face the Federal Court's final verdict today, ending a gruelling six-year legal battle over corruption-related charges. Until finally, in the Court of Appeal, the court ruled with a unanimous decision. Syed Saddiq will know whether he remains a free man or faces jail time over corruption-related charges when the Federal Court delivers its decision today.

items.title: US court allows tallying of mail-in ballots after Election Day
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Supreme Court, in a blow to President Donald Trump, upheld on Monday a state law that allows mail-in ballots received after Election Day to be counted. The court, in a 5-4 ruling, rejected a Republican challenge to a Mississippi law that allows mail-in ballots to be tallied if they are postmarked on Election Day and arrive within five business days after the day of the vote. Around 30 states allow ballots from some absentee voters received after Election Day to be counted.

items.title: Ringgit higher against greenback in early trade, tracks weaker US dollar index
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit was higher against the US dollar in the early session today, tracking a weaker US dollar index (DXY) as traders positioned ahead of key US labour market and consumer confidence data. At 8.03am, the local currency was pegged at 4.0605/0730 against the greenback from Monday's close of 4.0680/0735 Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US dollar remained at an elevated level in the absence of market-moving data points while the DXY stood at 101.10 points, declining by 0.25 per cent from the previous close. "Expect the ringgit to trade within a range of 4.06 to 4.08 as traders continue to observe the US data points.

items.title: Gold slips as US-Iran tensions spark inflation, rate hike fears
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday as fresh US-Iran tensions lifted oil prices and sparked fears of inflation, bolstering expectations of higher interest rates. Spot gold slid 1.7 per cent to US$4,020.68 per ounce by 2:00 p.m. US gold futures for August delivery settled 1.4 per cent lower at US$4,038.90.

items.title: [UPDATED] Federal Court verdict: Bella Astillah asks for prayers ahead of Syed Saddiq appeal decision [WATCH]
items.summary: She arrived ahead of the Federal Court's verdict on the prosecution's appeal to reinstate Syed Saddiq's seven-year jail sentence, two strokes of the rotan, and a RM10 million fine for corruption-related offences. Syed Saddiq, 34, had yet to be seen at the court complex at press time. Many media members arrived as early as 7am to await the court's decision.

items.title: Oil settles up on US-Iran strikes, cautious hopes for shipping cap gains
items.summary: HOUSTON: Oil prices settled up more than one per cent on Monday after attacks by the US and Iran underscored the fragility of their interim peace deal, while cautious hopes of a continued recovery in energy shipping through the Strait of Hormuz limited gains. Brent crude futures settled up US$1.16, or 1.61 per cent, at US$73.15 a barrel. US West Texas Intermediate crude gained US$1.52, or 2.2 per cent, to US$70.75.

items.title: Wall Street ends higher as US, Iran attacks ease
items.summary: NEW YORK: US stocks ended sharply higher on Monday, with the Dow hitting a record closing high, as weekend hostilities between the United States and Iran eased and as major technology-related shares rose following recent selling. "The fact that we had hostilities between the US and Iran over the weekend really didn't have a negative effect on the market," said Peter Cardillo, chief market economist at Spartan Capital Securities in New York. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 306.63 points, or 0.59 per cent, to 52,182.74, the S&P 500 gained 86.41 points, or 1.18 per cent, to 7,440.43 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 522.53 points, or 2.07 per cent, to 25,820.14.

items.title: Outgoing UK PM Starmer to unveil long-awaited defence investment plan
items.summary: LONDON: Outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Tuesday finally announce his long-delayed plan for funding Britain's future defence needs, with increased money for drones and autonomous systems. Two defence ministers quit earlier this month in a row over the Defence Investment Plan (DIP), including Defence Secretary John Healey, who said it risked making Britain "less safe". The resignations highlighted Starmer's weakening authority.

items.title: Money no longer buys employee loyalty
items.summary: FOR decades, organisations have relied on hierarchies, supervision and financial incentives to drive employee performance. Employees do not leave solely because of money. Pink proposed three key drivers of motivation: autonomy, mastery and purpose.

items.title: Bursa faces fresh questions over ability to retain quality listings [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ajinomoto (M) Bhd's proposed privatisation has renewed concerns over Bursa Malaysia's ability to retain quality listed companies. Market observers said the exercise highlights deeper structural issues facing the local equity market. Although the move is a company-specific corporate exercise, analysts said it underscores longstanding challenges related to liquidity, investor participation and market valuation, particularly among fundamentally strong but less actively traded companies.

items.title: Couple detained in France after twins die of dehydration
items.summary: LILLE, France: French police on Monday detained a couple after their 15-month-old twin daughters died of suspected dehydration, a source close to the investigation said. The couple's four other children, aged three, four, five and six, were hospitalised with dehydration but were not in life-threatening condition, the source added. Preliminary findings indicate that the children died of dehydration.

items.title: Doctors in England accept pay deal to end strikes
items.summary: LONDON: Doctors in England have voted to accept a pay and conditions offer, the UK government announced on Monday, bringing to an end a year of strike action. The so-called resident doctors – those below consultant level – have accepted an average 6.6 per cent pay uplift to be implemented by April 2027. Resident doctors have taken part in 21 days of strike action since July last year.

items.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo spreads to fourth province
items.summary: KINSHASA: A deadly Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has spread to a fourth province, meaning the country's entire northeast – home to around 15 million people – is now affected. The epidemic has claimed 360 lives out of 1,274 confirmed cases, according to figures from the World Health Organization. The conflict-hit Ituri province is the epicentre of the latest outbreak, reporting 1,165 cases and 301 deaths, according to health authorities.

items.title: 'I recognised her ring': identifying Venezuela's dead in a makeshift morgue
items.summary: LA GUAIRA: At a makeshift morgue in the quake-hit Venezuelan port of La Guaira, forensic experts in blue gowns and caps picked their way through dozens of bodies laid out in bags under the sun. As the official death toll rose past 1,700 and hospital morgues overflowed, the dock has been transformed into a mortuary, dotted with body bags and coffins. Near a white tent, about a hundred empty urns awaited ashes of the dead after cremation.

items.title: Peruvian political heir Fujimori wins presidency
items.summary: LIMA: Peru's conservative president-elect Keiko Fujimori vowed Monday to restore "order and hope" after defeating left-winger Roberto Sanchez in the latest victory for a resurgent Latin American right. The 51-year-old daughter of late president Alberto Fujimori secured the top office on her fourth attempt. [[nid:1476410]] DARK MEMORIES Fujimori will take office on July 28 for a five-year term.

items.title: Johor polls could push Bersatu to the brink of irrelevance [WATCH]
items.summary: However, the instruction carries deeper consequences for Bersatu's political future. Moreover, Bersatu entered the state election weakened after Pas decided on June 8 to sever their political cooperation. Endau is one of only two seats that Bersatu won, apart from Bukit Kepong, in the 2022 state election.

items.title: Moment of truth as Federal Court rules on prosecution’s challenge to Syed Saddiq’s acquittal today
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — For the past five years, Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has balanced two parallel lives: one as a prominent lawmaker, the other as the accused in one of Malaysia’s highest-profile political corruption trials. Today, those two paths finally converge. The prosecution is seeking to overturn the Court of Appeal’s unanimous June 2025 decision which acquitted the former youth and sports minister of four charges involving funds linked to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s (Bersatu) youth wing, Armada.

items.title: Malaysia’s nuclear footprint is bigger than many realise, stretching from rice fields to cancer wards
items.summary: The answer, according to the National Nuclear Technology Policy (DTNN) 2030, is that Malaysia already possesses a sizeable nuclear ecosystem — just not one centred on electricity generation. In many ways, Malaysia’s nuclear journey began decades ago. Malaysia already possesses a sizeable nuclear ecosystem — just not one centred on electricity generation.

items.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Cook but expands presidential powers
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Supreme Court refused on Monday to let Donald Trump fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook as it stood firm to preserve the central bank's cherished independence against an unprecedented challenge by the Republican president. The court, in a 5-4 ruling, blocked Trump from removing Cook for now, providing a safeguard for the Fed specifically, even as it boosted the president's power over government in a separate landmark ruling on Monday. No other president since the central bank's founding in 1913 had sought to oust a Fed governor.

items.title: Government extends MH370 search deal
items.summary: UNITED KINGDOM-based Ocean Infinity will continue the underwater search for Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 in the southern Indian Ocean by another 12 months. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the cabinet approved the extension on June 26, with the renewed agreement taking effect from tomorrow. Loke said the extension would allow the UK firm to continue the mission in the remaining 7,428.54 sq km search area.

items.title: Ministry may use AI to boost maritime security
items.summary: Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said the ministry would also expand the use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), including drones, with phased deployment planned across national waters, including Langkawi. He said UAVs would act as a force multiplier to existing maritime and patrol assets by enabling real-time aerial surveillance, particularly over vast, high-risk areas with limited monitoring coverage. Shamsul Anuar said the initiatives were in line with the ministry's Strategic Plan 2026-2030 and AI Strategic Plan 2026-2030.

items.title: Agrobank brings cashless drive to Pulau Perhentian
items.summary: PULAU PERHENTIAN: Agrobank has expanded its digital financial inclusion initiative with the launch of the Financial Education Network (FEN) eCelik Wang Carnival. This initiative aims to strengthen financial literacy and accelerate cashless payment adoption among island communities and micro-entrepreneurs. The development financial institution said the programme supports the National Strategy for Financial Literacy 2026-2030, particularly efforts to boost digital capabilities and encourage the responsible use of digital financial services.

items.title: Putin to press on with war goals
items.summary: PRESIDENT Vladimir Putin said on Sunday Russia will press ahead with its battlefield aim of fully capturing four Ukrainian regions, rejecting what he said was a new proposal by Ukraine to rein in hostilities in the more than four-year-old war. Putin, speaking to a Russian state television interviewer, also said Russia needed to boost its air defence capacity to counter intensified Ukrainian drone attacks aimed mainly at its oil industry. He said Russia was also coping well in tackling fuel supply problems linked to the Ukrainian strikes.

items.title: Fines to double for flouting under-16 ban
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia will double the financial penalty on platforms flouting its world-leading social media ban for under-16s to stem widespread evasion of the restrictions, the government said on Saturday. The independent regulator was "actively investigating" potential non-compliance by Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube, the government said. "It's clear big tech are not doing enough to comply with the law — there are still too many children on social media," Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said.

items.title: Consulate: Malaysians told to be on alert amid heavy rain warning in southern Thailand
items.summary: SONGKHLA, June 29 — The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla is urging Malaysians in southern Thailand and those travelling to the province to be on alert, following a heavy to very heavy rain forecast in several areas of the country from June 28 to July 3. Malaysian citizens requiring consular assistance may contact the duty officer at the Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla by calling +66 81-990-1930 or e-mailing mwsongkhla@kln.gov.my. The Malaysian Consulate General in Songkhla also informed that it would continue to monitor the weather and situational developments in Thailand and convey the latest information as required.

items.title: PM: 25,000 Malaysians will benefit from new Bakat Madani initiative
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 29 — A total of 25,000 people are set to benefit from the Bakat Madani initiative launched today, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the initiative is a national effort involving government-linked investment companies (GLICs), government-linked companies (GLCs), and Petronas to boost national talent development, focusing on skills development, the creation of quality jobs and improving social mobility. Meanwhile, Amir Hamzah said the initiative emphasises three main areas: strengthening employability and career placement within the GLIC, GLC and Petronas ecosystem; expanding job placement opportunities across key strategic sectors; and empowering Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions.

items.title: PM: Malaysia, Indonesia committed to strengthening cooperation in legislation, governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Malaysia and Indonesia are committed to strengthening cooperation in the fields of legislation, governance and various strategic areas of mutual interest, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he discussed the matter with Indonesia’s Coordinating Minister for Legal, Human Rights, Immigration and Correction, Prof Dr Yusril Ihza Mahendra, who paid him a courtesy call at the Prime Minister’s Office in Putrajaya today. During the meeting, Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said both leaders also exchanged views on efforts to strengthen cooperation in those fields.

items.title: Couple hospitalised after wild mushroom meal in Padang Besar
items.summary: SIK, June 29 — A husband and wife from Padang Besar were rushed to hospital after suffering severe food‑poisoning symptoms believed to be caused by eating wild mushrooms picked near a cattle shed at their home. Berita Harian reported that the couple, Muhd Basir Abidudin Abd Razak and Intan Nabila Mazelam, both 28, reportedly cooked the mushrooms for lunch before collapsing hours later. The mushrooms were later determined to be poisonous.

items.title: After viral Sabah hospital post, Sabah Health Dept cites familiar strain and overcrowding
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 29 — Sabah's Health Department has responded to a viral Facebook post depicting the crowded conditions at Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH), highlighting the overstretched public hospital’s infrastructure and manpower. The statement came after a Facebook user named Sualim Gopog shared his experience accompanying a family member to the hospital's Emergency and Trauma Department (ETD) in the early hours of the morning including slow moving queues and many people found sleeping in the lobby and outside the hospital building. In the widely shared post , Gopog described seeing dozens of people sleeping on benches and on the floor of the corridors, relatives searching for food after the hospital canteen closed at 12am, near-impossible parking, and patients waiting hours to be seen.

items.title: Bomb blast in Narathiwat: Wisma Putra confirms no Malaysian casualties
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 29 — Malaysia’s Foreign Ministry is monitoring developments in southern Thailand after an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in Narathiwat province today, with a Malaysian‑registered vehicle reportedly caught in the blast. The explosion occurred at about 11.45am local time, according to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA). In a preliminary update, Wisma Putra said no Malaysian fatalities have been reported, and the Consulate General in Songkhla is in contact with Thai authorities to verify details and extend consular assistance where needed.

items.title: Can-meh? Yes, new MEH licence plates for Melaka will open for bidding from July 10
items.summary: MELAKA, June 29 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) Melaka has issued a public notice encouraging Malaysians to participate in its latest round of vehicle registration number bidding via the JPJ eBid online platform for plates starting with ‘MEH’ In a Facebook post today, JPJ Melaka urged motorists and vehicle owners to register and submit bids through jpjebid.jpj.gov.my, the department’s official digital bidding system. Bids for MEH plates will start July 10 and close on July 14, with bid results announced on July 15. JPJ eBid, introduced nationwide in recent years, allows users to bid for preferred number plates without visiting JPJ counters.

items.title: High Court rejects former directors’ bid to delay RM492,480 refund owed to umrah pilgrims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The High Court here today dismissed an application by three former directors and shareholders of a tourism company seeking to postpone repayment of RM492,480 to umrah pilgrims pending the outcome of their appeal. Judge Leong Wai Hong dismissed the application by Datuk Dr Fathul Bari Mat Jahya, Sekh Mohd Fazzli Sekh Mohd Ruzi and Wan Azizul Wan Yusoff to stay execution of the payment order. Lawyer Mohd Shahril Madisa represented the three applicants at the proceedings, while lawyer Teoh Bi Shan appeared for KRS Travel Sdn Bhd (KRS), the company that manages the pilgrims’ travel to Makkah.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan says government finalising Engineers Scale of Fees review to set fairer pay benchmark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government is finalising a review of the Engineers Scale of Fees, which was gazetted in 1998, to ensure that professional engineering service fees are more in line with current economic realities and the operating costs of engineering consultancy firms. He said the amendments would replace the existing maximum fee structure with a new minimum fee benchmark to ensure that engineers receive professional remuneration comparable to that of other professionals in the construction industry. Ahmad said that once gazetted, the revised fee scale will be mandatory for all professional engineers and engineering consultancy practices (ECPs) operating in both the public and private sectors.

items.title: Fire tears through Subang Jaya apartment, four residents rescued after being trapped by thick smoke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Four residents were rescued after a fire gutted a first-floor apartment at Subang Perdana Goodyear Court 7 in USJ today, trapping occupants in an upper-floor unit due to thick smoke. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call at 10.02am and arrived at the apartment block in Jalan USJ 14/1 about 10 minutes later, reported Harian Metro . The fire destroyed an estimated 80 per cent of the first-floor apartment.

items.title: New MOH rule compels medicine suppliers to report shortages from July 1, ensuring stability despite West Asia risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Effective July 1, the Health Ministry (MOH) will enforce mandatory reporting by Product Registration Holders (PRHs) on any disruptions or discontinuation of medicine supply. The ministry said the mechanism is part of the proactive measures being taken to ensure the stability and control of the country’s imported medicine supply chain, despite risks arising from conflicts in West Asia. On medicine security in Sabah’s public healthcare services, the ministry said supply levels in the state remain stable and under control, despite unique logistical challenges due to geographical factors.

items.title: Dungun man accused of hammer attack on grandmother ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 29 — The Sessions Court today ordered a 25-year-old man charged with causing grievous hurt to his grandmother with a hammer to undergo a mental health assessment. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli issued the order against Muhammad Nor Iman Abdullah following an application by the prosecution, which requested that the accused undergo a psychiatric evaluation before any plea or statement is recorded. The court ordered him to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak, to undergo a mental assessment.

items.title: Azalina tables sweeping amendment to Act 792 expanding child sex crime law and closing loopholes overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The proposed amendment to the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 (Act 792) seeks to ensure that Malaysia does not face jurisdictional constraints in taking action against perpetrators of child sexual crimes, even if the offences are committed abroad. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the move is in line with efforts to strengthen protection for children against cross-border sexual crimes, while ensuring the act remains relevant amid the evolving nature of sexual offences. “At the same time, protection is also extended to children who are Malaysian citizens, permanent residents, or ordinarily reside in Malaysia,” she said.

items.title: Two Malaysians injured after IED blast near SUV in Thailand’s Tak Bai, one seriously hurt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Two Malaysians were injured after an improvised explosive device (IED) planted beneath a roadside culvert exploded near their vehicle in Thailand’s Tak Bai district this afternoon, with one of the victims suffering serious injuries. According to Berita Harian, Abdullah suffered shrapnel wounds to his forehead, serious injuries to his left leg and head injuries believed to have been caused by shattered glass, while Muhammad Yusri sustained minor injuries. “The victims’ vehicle was also damaged in the bomb blast.

items.title: Three young Malaysian scientists head to Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, joining 600 peers worldwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Three young scientists have been selected to represent the country at the 75th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting, which is being held in Lindau, Germany, from June 28 to July 3. The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti), in a statement today, announced that the selected representatives are Department of Pharmacology lecturer at the Faculty of Medicine, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM), Dr Nur Liyana Mohammed Yusof, 34; postdoctoral research fellow at the Department of Physics, Universiti Malaya (UM), Dr Pershaanaa K. Manogran, 29; and postdoctoral fellow at UM Institute of Ocean and Earth Sciences, Dr Ruth Naomi Manuel, 31. According to Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang, Malaysia’s participation in global scientific platforms such as the Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting reflects the government’s commitment to nurturing future-ready talent in line with the aspirations of the 13th Malaysia Plan (2026-2030).

items.title: Puad claims secret Umno‑PAS talks bred overconfidence, now BN faces precarious situation
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Former Johor assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi claimed that the secret plot between Umno and PAS to form a political partnership for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections had led to the former being conceited. Puad, who was a former Umno supreme council member, revealed that the initial agreement required PAS to sit out the Johor state election, paving the way for a Barisan Nasional (BN) victory in all 56 constituencies. “Among the Umno leaders, Datuk Megat (political secretary to the deputy prime minister Datuk Wira Megat Zulkarnain Omardin) believed that if the negotiations were successful Umno can win big in Johor.

items.title: Dzulkefly tells Dewan Rakyat MOH reviewing cerebral palsy registry plan to widen rehab access
items.summary: Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the proposal is aimed at expanding patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) rehabilitation centres. “MOH welcomes the proposal to establish a National Cerebral Palsy Registry and is also reviewing efforts to expand patients’ access to rehabilitation facilities, including through collaboration with Perkeso rehabilitation centres,” he said when replying to a question from Dr Kelvin Yii Lee Wuen (PH-Bandar Kuching). Dzulkefly said MOH assists CP patients in obtaining rehabilitation equipment through the Medical Assistance Fund, the Malaysian Medical Welfare Fund and the National Cancer Council, based on clinical assessment and patients’ eligibility.

items.title: Cabinet greenlights MH370 deep-sea search extension with Ocean Infinity for another year, search to resume July 1, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The government has agreed to extend its agreement with UK-based Ocean Infinity for another 12 months to continue deep-sea efforts to locate Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 in the southern Indian Ocean. Transport Minister Loke Siew Fook said the Cabinet approved the extension on June 26, with the new contract period running from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2027. Loke said the decision also takes into account Ocean Infinity’s new commercial commitments, which require its main operational assets to be temporarily redeployed elsewhere.

items.title: Ex‑Temerloh police chief ordered to enter defence on 46 bribery charges totalling RM69,600
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 29 — The Sessions Court here today ordered former Temerloh district police chief Mohd Azhar Mohd Yusoff to enter his defence on 46 counts of accepting bribes totalling RM69,600 between 2019 and 2023. “The court finds and rules that the prosecution has successfully established a prima facie case on all 46 principal charges faced by the accused under Section 165 of the Penal Code. Accordingly, the court ordered Mohd Azhar to enter his defence on all the charges.
